globalRules: The first hands-on repository exercise starts from an ordinary folder and requires the learner to run git init -b main.
Hidden fixture setup runs automatically and must never appear in learner-facing prompts, hints, starterCode, solutionCode, terminal expectations, or terminal history.
Every technical lesson after initialization begins from deterministic prerequisite repository state and asks the learner to perform only the newly taught Git action.
Final learner quiz cards contain no code_input. quizDraft remains the authoring collection for runtime Try It exercises.
Every standard module contains exactly one role=module_project section.
The final module contains exactly one role=capstone section and one capstone topic.
Every project step N+1 starts from the complete repository state produced by step N.
No exercise requires branch, remote, or hosted collaboration knowledge.

forbidden: commits beyond the initial project workflow
